key_insight: PNOT reconstructs divertor temperature fields by combining graph-based boundary physics, physics-aware operator attention, and gradient-constrained learning to preserve both accuracy and physical consistency.
engineering_relevance: This enables faster, real-time thermal monitoring and control for tungsten monoblock divertors, helping reduce melting risk and extend component lifetime in fusion devices.
method: Models boundary heat-flux relations as a structured graph
Uses graph attention to capture spatial physical dependencies
Adds physics-aware neural operator slicing and Sobolev regularization to enforce diffusion behavior and derivative consistency
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that experiments show the added physical constraints improve prediction accuracy while maintaining physical consistency.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality of boundary-condition graphs and careful tuning of physics-aware attention and regularization terms for new operating regimes.
paper_title: Temperature Field Reconstruction of Tungsten Monoblock Divertor on EAST using Physics-aware Neural Operator Transformer
